exercises.title: ft_putchar
exercises.description:
Write a function that displays a character on the standard output.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_putchar(char c);
```
**Constraints:**
You can only use `write` from <unistd.h>.

exercises.title: ft_print_alphabet
exercises.description:
Write a function that prints the alphabet in lowercase, followed by a newline.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_alphabet(void);
```

exercises.title: ft_print_reverse_alphabet
exercises.description:
Write a function that prints the alphabet in reverse lowercase order, followed by a newline.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_reverse_alphabet(void);
```

exercises.title: ft_print_numbers
exercises.description:
Write a function that prints all digits from 0 to 9.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_numbers(void);
```

exercises.title: ft_is_negative
exercises.description:
Write a function that displays 'N' if the number is negative and 'P' if positive or zero.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_is_negative(int n);
```

exercises.title: ft_print_comb
exercises.description:
Write a function that displays all different combinations of three digits in ascending order.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_comb(void);
```

exercises.title: ft_print_comb2
exercises.description:
Write a function that displays all combinations of two two-digit numbers.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_comb2(void);
```

exercises.title: ft_putnbr
exercises.description:
Write a function that prints an integer.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_putnbr(int nb);
```

exercises.title: ft_print_combn
exercises.description:
Write a function that displays all combinations of `n` different digits in ascending order.

**Function prototype:**
```c
void ft_print_combn(int n);
```

exercises.title: ft_strcpy
exercises.description:
Reproduce the behavior of the function `strcpy`.

**Function prototype:**
```c
char *ft_strcpy(char *dest, char *src);
```

exercises.title: ft_strncpy
exercises.description:
Reproduce the behavior of the function `strncpy`.

**Function prototype:**
```c
char *ft_strncpy(char *dest, char *src, unsigned int n);
```

exercises.title: ft_str_is_alpha
exercises.description:
Return 1 if the string contains only alphabetical characters, 0 otherwise.

**Function prototype:**
```c
int ft_str_is_alpha(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_str_is_numeric
exercises.description:
Return 1 if the string contains only numeric characters, 0 otherwise.

**Function prototype:**
```c
int ft_str_is_numeric(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_str_is_lowercase
exercises.description:
Return 1 if the string contains only lowercase letters, 0 otherwise.

**Function prototype:**
```c
int ft_str_is_lowercase(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_str_is_uppercase
exercises.description:
Return 1 if the string contains only uppercase letters, 0 otherwise.

**Function prototype:**
```c
int ft_str_is_uppercase(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_str_is_printable
exercises.description:
Return 1 if the string contains only printable characters, 0 otherwise.

**Function prototype:**
```c
int ft_str_is_printable(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_strupcase
exercises.description:
Transform all lowercase letters to uppercase in the string.

**Function prototype:**
```c
char *ft_strupcase(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_strlowcase
exercises.description:
Transform all uppercase letters to lowercase in the string.

**Function prototype:**
```c
char *ft_strlowcase(char *str);
```

exercises.title: ft_strcapitalize
exercises.description:
Capitalize the first letter of each word and lowercase the others.

**Function prototype:**
```c
char *ft_strcapitalize(char *str);
```
